title: system service: unset NOTIFY_SOCKET
body:
Unset the NOTIFY_SOCKET environment variable after sending the MAIN_PID and READY message.  This avoids any unintentional side-effects of other code paths using the socket assuming they'd run in a non-server short-lived Podman process.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
When running under systemd (e.g., via Quadlet), Podman will extend the start timeout in 30s steps up to 5min max. when pulling an image.
```

comments.body: Should add tests for it.
LGTM

@edsantiago PTAL
To prevent a heart attack: I am not sure whether you like the GDB trick but I didn't find a more "straight-forward" solution to list the _current_ environment of another process. /proc/$pid/environ lists the env at process creation which is outdated.

Requiring gdb means that will be a new dependency that everyone (gating, CI, local) needs to have installed. I don't think it is important to actually the test the env here.
Couldn't you just start a container with --sdnotify=container via the API and make sure the socket is not leaked into the container?  Because the service unset the env var so there is nothing to leak in such case and IMO that would be the important bug fix that this fixes.

> Couldn't you just start a container with --sdnotify=container via the API and make sure the socket is not leaked into the container?

That's less fun but definitely the better option, thanks!

> That's less fun but definitely the better option, thanks!

Turns out `--sdnotify=container` never worked on remote.  It doesn't make sense fore remote but I somehow expected it to break without this patch.

This is a toughie. FWIW I tried something like
* start server
* podman-remote run -d ... top
* podman inspect that container, get conmon pid
* tr '\0' '\012' /proc/conmonpid/env looking for NOTIFY_SOCKET

Nope, NOTIFY_SOCKET doesn't get to conmon, nor to the podman process either. (This is with regular podman, sans your patch. I wanted to see how to test this).

Got nowhere, and I need to give up for today. Will look again on Monday.

(Oh, and, yeah, let's try everything possible before resorting to gdb please)

@edsantiago let's wait for https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1616 to progress.  In case we move forward with https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1616, we can test unsetting NOTIFY_SOCKET more easily.  Namely, an `image pull` would not yield a specific DBUS message on the remote server.
@edsantiago WDYT of the new tests?
@vrothberg, sorry, what new tests? I don't see a new push?
The heat must be killing me at the moment - I was convinced of having pushed yesterday evening.  I'll push in a jiffy.
The jiffy got boiled as well :^)  New tests are up.
@vrothberg is it safe to assume that you're looking into and resolving the `USEC` failures?

> @vrothberg is it safe to assume that you're looking into and resolving the `USEC` failures?

Thanks!  Yes, I am on it.  Will push in $jiffy.
Should pass now.  Needed to make https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1616 a bit smarter as it was sending the USEC on --pull=newer even when the image was up to date.

@edsantiago the APIv2 errors below look like flakes BUT `exist.io` actually exists, so I think the test should use localhost instead.

> [+0154s] not ok 1170 [60-auth] POST /v1.40/auth [-d {"username":"u9nBV48a","password":"WrOnGPassWord","serveraddress":"does.not.exist.io:1234/"}] : .message
[+0154s] #  expected: ~ .*no such host.*
[+0154s] #    actual: login attempt to does.not.exist.io:1234/ failed with status: authenticating creds for "does.not.exist.io:1234": pinging container registry does.not.exist.io:1234: Get "https://does.not.exist.io:1234/v2/": dial tcp: lookup does.not.exist.io on 169.254.169.254:53: server misbehaving

I.... think maybe not? As I read the test, it is important for it to fail with "no such host". `localhost` won't give us that. (For a moment I was tempted to suggest `localhsot` but even I am not that cruel).

Maybe `nxhost.nxdomain`? Or `nosuchhost.podman.io`? But even if we change that, I don't see it helping this particular failure: "server misbehaving" is new to me, but AFAICT it would not matter _what_ nonexistenthost we used in this test, it would still fail the same way.

> `exist.io`

we can use something like `doesnotexists.podman.io` which is under our control
Rebased.  Ready for the final round of reviews.

LGTM minus a small typo that I'm not even going to point out because we need an excuse to run spellcheck once in a while.

@vrothberg why is gitbot auto-adding the WIP label?

> why is gitbot auto-adding the WIP label?

No idea. Maybe the api-chance implies WIP (would be strange)?
Should be good to merge :+1:

> LGTM minus a small typo that I'm not even going to point out because we need an excuse to run spellcheck once in a while.
> 
> @vrothberg why is gitbot auto-adding the WIP label?

TOCTOU race conditions in the usage of the github API. I am not sure whose fault that is but I seen it before, it happens if two applications update labels at the same time. You can even trigger it via the webui.
